element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns takeoff, ADF programming, navigation, and whisper mode landing without detection
Player must hail nearby planes and engage in combat if identification fails
Player learns to use papers to deceive guards and begins inventory management
Multi-room building navigation to find cabinet with security card to unlock doors
Find four containers, deliver to lab, identify nerve gas neutralizer, locate and neutralize vat
Used to incapacitate guards when papers fail to maintain cover
Used to discover buried mines while navigating ground compounds
Locate Dr. Phineas Gump, administer invisibility pill for escape
Plant in seven missile control rooms before 10-minute timer expires
20-minute real-time countdown after planting explosives, must reach helicopter

typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure (20-minute escape timer in final mission)
Return flight with reduced enemy opposition
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycles of high-tension flight segments followed by stealth-based ground infiltration with moderate risk escalation through mission progression

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Security cards hidden in cabinets within multi-room buildings encourage exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate introduction to helicopter flight mechanics and basic infiltration with papers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased complexity with objective variants (chemical container identification, scientist rescue) requiring more deliberate navigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: High tension with timed escape sequence and multiple missile control room targets requiring systematic coverage

notable_level_patterns.description: Six missions arranged in three pairs: odd-numbered helicopter flight missions (1, 3, 5) followed by ground-based infiltration missions (2, 4, 6). Return flights are shorter with reduced enemy opposition.
Ground missions take place across multi-room buildings where player must navigate rooms to locate objectives (security cards, containers, personnel) hidden in cabinets or locations
Mission 2 introduces basic card-finding, Mission 4 adds complexity with rescue and item administration, Mission 6 demands systematic target coverage under time pressure
Ground missions rely on papers for guard deception, with escalating failure consequences requiring gas canisters or alternative approaches as alertness increases
